title: Skillet Chicken Thighs
imagePrompt: Bone-in chicken thighs sit skin-side up in a dark cast iron skillet, their skin deep golden-brown and blistered crisp. A pale ivory cream sauce pools around the thighs and clings to the crispy skin in a thin glaze, flecked with minced shallot and garlic and a few thyme leaves. A scatter of chopped parsley sits bright green across the top, with the glossy sauce visibly thinner and looser where it pools at the edges of the pan.
subtitle: Crispy-skinned chicken thighs in a rich garlic-shallot cream sauce, all in one skillet.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Sauce is a light cream pan sauce built from the fond—not a heavy gravy.
items: 6 chicken thighs (bone-in, skin-on, about 1½ lbs)
½ tsp garlic powder
½ tsp onion powder
½ tsp paprika
Kosher salt and black pepper, to taste
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tbsp butter
1 small shallot, minced
3 cloves garlic, minced
½ cup chicken broth
1 tbsp fresh lemon juice
2 sprigs fresh thyme
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
⅓ cup heavy cream
Freshly chopped parsley, for garnish
ingredients.callout: Key rule: Let the skin sear undisturbed—moving it too early tears the crust and it won't crisp back up.

body.html: Bone-in thighs seared skin-side down until deeply golden and crispy, then finished gently until cooked through. The skillet drippings become a quick garlic-shallot cream sauce spooned right back over the chicken.

items: Dry + season chicken thighs
Sear skin-side down until crispy—don't move it
Flip + finish cooking to 165°F
Build sauce from the fond: shallot, garlic, broth, cream
Return chicken to pan, garnish with parsley

body.html: Lesson learned: A cold, dry skillet start won't crisp skin—get the pan properly hot with oil shimmering before the chicken goes in.

items: Pat chicken thighs very dry with paper towels, skin included.
Combine garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
Season both sides of the chicken generously.

body.html: Why dry matters: Moisture on the skin steams instead of crisping. Dry skin browns fast and evenly.

items: Heat a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Once shimmering, place chicken skin-side down in a single layer.
Cook undisturbed for 7–8 minutes, until the skin is deep golden and crispy.

body.html: Don't move it. Pressing or shifting the chicken while it sears tears the crust before it releases naturally.

items: Flip the chicken and reduce heat to medium.
Cook 15–25 minutes, until internal temperature reaches 165°F at the thickest part.
Transfer chicken to a plate and set aside.

body.html: Doneness cue: Juices should run clear and the thickest part should read 165°F on an instant-read thermometer—time varies with thigh size.

items: Add butter, shallot, and garlic to the skillet. Cook about 1 minute, stirring, until fragrant.
Stir in the chicken broth, lemon juice, thyme, and red pepper flakes.
Scrape the pan to release the browned bits from searing.

body.html: Scraping the pan is important. Those browned bits are the backbone of the sauce's flavor.

slides.title: Stir in Cream + Simmer
slides.checkboxLabel: Low heat, stir in cream, simmer to thicken

items: Reduce heat to low.
Stir in the heavy cream.
Simmer about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly.

body.html: Target texture: Light enough to coat a spoon, not thick like gravy. Low heat keeps the cream from separating.

slides.title: Return Chicken + Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Nestle chicken back in, garnish, serve

items: Return the chicken thighs to the skillet, nestling them into the sauce.
Spoon sauce over the top to warm through.
Garnish with freshly chopped parsley and serve warm.

body.html: Keep skin up. Nestle the thighs skin-side up in the sauce so the crispy skin stays crispy instead of soaking.
